blocks:
Sūrat An-Naṣr

Medinan, and it consists of three verses by consensus. بِسْمِ اللهِ الرَّحْمنِ الرَّحِيمِ (In the Name of Allah, the All-beneficent, the All-merciful)

(١) إِذا جاءَ نَصْرُ اللهِ ** (meaning) to you against your enemies وَالْفَتْحُ ** (and the victory)—the conquest of مكّة. (When the help of Allah comes)

(٢) وَرَأَيْتَ النَّاسَ يَدْخُلُونَ فِي دِينِ اللهِ أَفْواجاً ** in groups, like the people of مكّة and الطائف and اليمن and the rest of the Arab tribes. (and you see the people entering the religion of Allah in throngs,)

(٣) فَسَبِّحْ بِحَمْدِ رَبِّكَ ** So glorify Him, praising Him for having fulfilled His promise وَاسْتَغْفِرْهُ ** (and plead with Him for forgiveness)—out of humility for yourself or for your nation (أمّتك)—إِنَّهُ كانَ تَوَّاباً (Indeed He is all-clement.) (then celebrate the praise of your Lord, and plead with Him for forgiveness. Indeed He is all-clement.)

Al-Qummi said: It was revealed in منى during the Farewell Pilgrimage (حجّة الوداع). When it was revealed, the Messenger of Allah صلى الله عليه وآله وسلم said: "My death has been announced to me." It was said that perhaps this was because it indicated the completion of the call (الدعوة) and the perfection of the matter of the religion (الدّين).

In al-Kafi and al-Uyun, narrated from al-Sadiq عليه السلام: Indeed, the first thing that was revealed was اقْرَأْ بِاسْمِ رَبِّكَ ** (Read in the Name of your Lord), and the last of it was إِذا جاءَ نَصْرُ اللهِ** (When the help of Allah comes).

In al-Kafi, narrated from Umm Salamah, who said: In the last of his days, the Messenger of Allah صلى الله عليه وآله وسلم would not stand up, nor sit down, nor come, nor go, except that He said: "Glory be to Allah and praise be to Him; I seek forgiveness from Allah and I turn to Him in repentance." So we asked Him about that, and He said: "I was commanded to do so," and then He recited this Sūrah.

In Thawab al-A'mal and al-Majma', narrated from al-Sadiq عليه السلام: Whoever recites إِذا جاءَ نَصْرُ اللهِ (When the help of Allah comes) in an obligatory prayer (فريضة) or a supererogatory prayer (نافلة), Allah will grant him victory over all his enemies. And he will come on the Day of Resurrection with a speaking book that Allah has brought forth from the depths of his grave. In it is security (أمان) from the bridge of Hell (جهنّم), from the Fire, and from the roaring of Hell (زفير جهنّم). He will not pass by anything on the Day of Resurrection except that it gives him glad tidings and informs him of every good, until he enters Paradise (الجنّة). And in this world, He will open for him such means of goodness as he had neither wished for nor crossed his mind.
